fields.Description: At the first sTOUdio DATE at Celje Youth Centre (MCC) in January 2020, we discussed the need for new approaches to digital content marketing with knowledgeable current/former students. Not to be in an all-male society, it was fortunate that the MCC receptionist was a Turistica graduate with an extended internship. On leaving, we also encouraged her: to write a final thesis on the topic of the impact of digital recommendations in tourism under the supervision of UP FTŠ and sTOUdio expert - Miha Bratec.
fields.Single line text: V Celjskem mladinskem centru (MCC) smo izvedli prvi sTOUdio zmenek, kjer smo z razgledanimi sedanjimi in bivšimi študenti razpravljali o nujnosti novih pristopov k digitalnemu marketingu.
fields.Single line ENG: We held our first sTOUdio date at Celje Youth Centre (MCC), where we discussed the need for new approaches to digital content marketing with knowledgeable current and former students.

fields.Description:
The mentors of the international initiatives www.TourismFromZero.org and www.AIRTH.global, which collect and support ideas on how to support tourism and its innovations during and after the pandemic, have endorsed a proposal by a group of sTOUdio students at UP FTŠ Turistica. This is the #LocalsFromZero experience platform with a deep social touch.

On the one hand, the platform provides education and assistance in the digitalization of smaller, authentic tourism operators, and on the other hand, it allows students to gain experience in the role of an LFZ Scout. The role of an LFZ Scout is to gather business advice and manage booking platforms and digital markets. There are currently 47 active experiences and 23 Scouts on the platform. The Scouts look after the experiences and look for new opportunities for new experiences in their local area. Good words about this spread in Istria, Croatia, where students Tadej Rogelja and Rudi Medved had a presentation of the initiative and impressed the first Scouts from Croatia at the University of Pula (www.unipu.hr).

Last year the idea got a legal status (the students founded a legal entity). With the company recognized as very innovative by Dewesoft's entrepreneurial accelerator www.katapult.si, the students became incubators, receiving business advice from Slovenia's most successful entrepreneurs, such as Dr. Jure Knez (www.katapult.si/kdo-je-jure-knez/) and Andrej Orožen (www.linkedin.com/in/andrej-oro%C5%BEen-95373a55). 

In January 2021, the coordinators of the #LocalsFromZero initiative (most of whom attend various study programs at UP FTŠ Turistica) were announced as one of the five finalists of the IFITT international competition Innovation Challenge @ENTER21 (www.enter-conference.org/compete) under the wings of Global Ecotourism Network (www.globalecotourismnetwork.org) mentors. With their help, they updated their booking platform and added new content that will contribute to the development of local communities. The finalist status allowed them to present themselves at this year's most important conference in the field of e-tourism, ENTER 2021.
fields.Single line text: Študentski startup se je konec leta 2021 združil z mednarodno kooperativo Fairbnb.coop. Platforma za rezervacijo doživetij je bila v prvi vrsti zasnovana za zapostavljene periferne turistične ponudnike.
fields.Single line ENG: The student start-up merged with the international Fairbnb.coop at the end of 2021. The experience booking platform was primarily designed for neglected peripheral tourism providers.

fields.Description: In February 2020, we were on a sTOUdio date in the Karst. Tadej Rogelja invited us there after receiving his Master's degree from Herbal Glamping under the mentorship of Associate Professor Dr. Dejan Križaj. We visited him at his family’s herb farm  https://zeliscarogelja.si. There we listened to the presentation of his master's thesis, a short lecture on tourism trends in 2020 (Dejan Križaj, Miha Bratec), a short presentation of the herb farm (there was plenty of good food) and we also got to know the participants of the event. At the invitation of the host, we were welcomed by the head of the Institute for Tourism Miren-Kras, mag. Ariana Suhadolnik. She presented us her success stories of building a new destination brand.
fields.Single line text: Šli smo na sTOUdio zmenek na Kras, kamor nas je povabil Tadej Rogelja na obisk njegove družinske zeliščne kmetije, potem ko je magistriral na temo Herbal Glampinga.
fields.Single line ENG: We went on a sTOUdio date in the Karst, where we were invited by Tadej Rogelja to visit his family's herb farm after he received his Master's degree on the topic of Herbal Glamping.

fields.Description: The first full-day #sTOUdio trip took us to Maribor and its surroundings in March 2020. No less than 41 students learned about alternative forms of tourism guidance under the guidance of the Rajzefiber office (https://www.rajzefiber.si/) and in this way learned a lot about the Styrian metropolis. At Glamping Chocolate Village by the River (https://chocolatevillage.eu) we learned about innovative forms of boutique accommodation that build their added value on thematic experiences and what the values and characteristics of serial entrepreneurs are. The presentation of the activities of the Institute for Innovation and Entrepreneurship Maribor (http://www.zipiie.eu/slo/o-nas/index.html) showed all the complexity and number of challenges in implementing change in the local tourism environment.
fields.Single line text: Na prvem sTOUdio izletu smo obiskali Štajersko, kjer smo spoznali inovativne oblike butične namestitve in se seznanili s številnimi izzivi, s katerimi se sooča regija pri uvajanju sprememb v lokalnem turističnem okolju.
fields.Single line ENG: On the first sTOUdio trip we visited Štajerska, where we learned of innovative forms of boutique accommodation and were acquainted with the many challenges the region faces in implementing change in the local tourism environment.

fields.Description: In September 2020, we organized the second #sTOUdio trip. This time we went underground, even if only virtually, as we visited the Virtual Museum of Mining 4.dritl in Trbovlje, which belongs to the #LocalsFromZero family with its innovative experience. After visiting the museum, we tested new thematic tours of the city, which hides many interesting stories and intertwinements of the past with the present. To end the trip, we took the students to Kum or "Zasavje Triglav '' where we saw the nascent project of a young couple who want to renovate an almost 100 year old house and turn it into a tourist homestead. They were happy to listen to our advice and we were equally happy to sample some of their homemade treats.
fields.Single line text: Na našem drugem sTOUdio izletu smo obiskali Trbovlje, se seznanili z zgodovino rudarjenja in preizkusili nekaj tematskih ogledov mesta.
fields.Single line ENG: On our second sTOUdio trip we visited Trbovlje, learned of its history of mining and tested some thematic tours of the city.

fields.Description:
The first sTOUdio weekend took place in October 2019 at UP FTŠ Turistica, as part of the LAS Turizmo Ištriano project. Over 40 active and former students of UP FTŠ Turistica came together. The challenges that the students solved in groups were related to the development of new tourist products from Slovenian Istria and had to take into account all the current guidelines of the experience economy and sustainable development.

The following providers participated as challengers with their ideas and weekend challenges: 

http://lisjak.com/
https://www.istrianbreakfast.si/
http://vinska-fontana.si/
http://mytilus.si/

To begin, the students familiarized themselves with the basic trends and concepts of tourism product development and saw the current products and sites of challengers in the field and had informal discussions with them. In the development portion of the weekend, they developed creative new product solutions under the careful guidance of mentors using the "Lean Startup" methodology.

The professional management and smooth flow of the development of new product ideas was provided by a team consisting of: Dr. Dejan Križaj, Mag. Miha Bratec, Tamara Kozlovič, Mag. Tadej Rogelja. 

The Startup Weekend also had long-term effects, as some solutions impressed the challengers so much that even after the more than successful first sTOUdio weekend, they offered the students a collaboration for the implementation of the presented solutions in the form of student work!
fields.Single line text: Preko 40 aktivnih in bivših študentov UP FTŠ Turistica se je zbralo na prvem sTOUdio vikendu, kjer so reševali izzive v zvezi z razvojem novih turističnih produktov slovenske Istre.
fields.Single line ENG: Over 40 active and former students of UP FTŠ Turistica came together at the first sTOUdio weekend, where they solved challenges in relation to the development of new tourist products from Slovenian Istria.

fields.Description:
The second sTOUdio weekend took place in November 2020 under the pandemic circumstances of the unpredictable year 2020 and was therefore presented in a virtual environment. A special feature of the weekend was that the organizing team of students (who are also part of the #LocalsFromZero team) was responsible for the preparation and implementation of the event - including the selection, learning and management of technologies for the promotion and implementation of the online event.
Thematically, the sTOUdio weekend was dedicated to solving challenges related to the promotion and sale of wine, which is included in tourism as a complementary experience and gastronomy
At the event, student groups solved business challenges presented by winemakers and wine tourism-related organizations:

Klet Brda
Matjaževa domačija 
Santomas 
TIC Dutovlje – Visit Kras
Vina Prinčič 
Vina Jarc
Vina Bordon 
Institute of Tourism, Culture, Youth and Sports Brda.

Lecturers and mentors helped the student groups to solve the challenges:

Mateja Hrvatin Kozlovič – Hiške Slovenske Istre 
Janja Viher – Institute for Innovation and Entrepreneurship, Maribor 
Katja Ertl – TIC Ptuj – Visit Ptuj 
dr. Dejan Križaj – UP FTŠ Turistica in AIRTH 
dr. Simon Kerma – UP FTŠ Turistica 
Miha Bratec – UP FTŠ Turistica in AIRTH 
Tadej Rogelja – UP FTŠ Turistica in AIRTH  
Rudi Medved – Zavod Ideas From Zero 

On Sunday, students presented the proposed solutions to selected challengers and received various prizes for their efforts (and depending on the degree of satisfaction of the challengers with the presented solutions) - from bottles of wine to invitations to tastings, excursions and business meetings to overnight stays in vineyard cottages.
To make the startup event as real as possible for the participants, in addition to the prizes, the students received sponsor packages (goodie bags) in the mail before the event, which helped them overcome the mental efforts.
The main takeaway and positive experience from the event was that with good planning and a lot of creativity, it is possible to organize quality startup events in a very professional manner in the online environment. This was also confirmed by a number of challengers who praised the enviable level of professionalism and organization of the whole sTOUdio #WineFomZero 2020 weekend in the flood of different online events.
fields.Single line text: Drugi sTOUdio vikend je potekal v virtualnem okolju in je bil namenjen reševanju poslovnih izzivov, povezanih s promocijo in prodajo vina.
fields.Single line ENG: The second sTOUdio weekend took place in a virtual environment and was dedicated to solving business challenges related to the promotion and sale of wine.

fields.Description:
The #TourismFromZero podcast (https://podcast.TourismFromZero.org) is one of the ideas supported by the mentoring group of the #TourismFromZero initiative and was initiated by one of the coordinators of the #LocalsFromZero team (www.LocalsFromZero.org) and an MSc student at the FTŠ Turistica, Rudi Medved.

Like the most interesting experiences, the most interesting personal and professional experiences are offered by experts in the field. These experts are interviewed by Rudi Medved in the #TourismFromZero podcast. They share their personal experiences of how they contribute to the development of Slovenian and global tourism. The episodes, which last between 40and 60 minutes, are a new base of tourism and business knowledge, as the topics covered are related to the development of the tourism offer, the involvement of stakeholders in the destination, the management of the pandemic and much more.

27 episodes (six in English) have already been produced, which you can listen to on the #TourismFromZero website and in all major podcast clients. The topics covered in the podcast are useful and educational for anyone working in tourism. Above all, the podcast is well interwoven with our other activities and makes sense of what the #LocalsFromZero and sTOUdio Turistica teams are doing on the field.
fields.Single line text: Podkast #TourismFromZero je ena od idej, ki jih je podprla mentorska skupina iniciative #TourismFromZero in je nastal na pobudo enega od koordinatorjev ekipe #LocalsFromZero in magistrskega študenta FTŠ Turistice, Rudija Medveda.
fields.Single line ENG: The #TourismFromZero podcast is one of the ideas supported by the mentoring group of the #TourismFromZero initiative and was initiated by one of the coordinators of the #LocalsFromZero team and an MSc student at the FTŠ Turistica, Rudi Medved.

fields.Description:
The village of Marezige above Koper is the capital of Refošk. In honor of this, the first Slovenian wine fountain was opened in the village. This unique attraction is perfect for all gourmets and lovers of good wine. Moreover, Marezige is an excellent starting point for exploring the Istrian countryside.

The CEO of Marezige Wine Fountain, Mr. Alen Babič, visited the 2019 sTOUdio weekend as a challenger. He first entertained the students and introduced the operation of Wine Fountain and then challenged them to come up with an idea to add to the tourist offer during the weekend. At the end of the weekend, the Turistica students presented the idea of creating a photo point on the Marezige observation deck. The Marezige wine fountain has one of the most beautiful views on the entire coast. The students saw an opportunity to increase the online presence of Wine Fountain. This would be done by placing a sculpture of two wine glasses to serve as a photo point for passersby. Mr Babič expressed interest in further collaboration and so three 3rd year students (Jaka Godejša, Marcel Višner and Ajda Medvešek) from University Tourism Program developed the idea further.

The project came closer and closer to realization. Arrangements were already made with the producer of the wine glass sculpture. Before the project entered the next phase, the students together with their mentor dr. Križaj decided that it would be best to get an additional expert opinion. So, at the very beginning of the epidemic in 2020, we conducted an online web meeting between the students of Turistica, the manager of Marezige Wine Fountain and the landscape architect Romana Kačič.

The event proved to be very important for the development of the project and once again confirmed the importance of interdisciplinary cooperation between different professions and sectors in tourism. Ms Romana Kačič comprehensively presented her insight into the Marezige viewing platform. She gave a set of guidelines that generally need to be taken into account when placing tourism products in the space and steered the idea group on the right path. She confronted us with findings that we had not encountered before in tourism research and that we had not taken into account in our conception. We believe that such events, which provide new technical insights and context, should become a regular practice for other projects in academia and industry, as we too often involve experts from different relevant disciplines in the conceptualization process.

The epidemic hampered further work on the project, but the team at Wine Fountain did not give up. The project was supported by the Participatory Budget of the municipality of Koper and in April 2022 was implemented. Since then many guests have documented their visit by taking a photo of the stunning glasses.
fields.Single line text: Idejna zasnova za novo turistično atrakcijo v Marezigah je nastala na sTOUdio vikendu oktobra 2019. Aprila 2022 smo ponosno otovorili novo pridobitev.
fields.Single line ENG: The conceptual design for the new tourist attraction in Marezige was created at the sTOUdio weekend in October 2019. In April 2022, we proudly unveiled the new attraction.

fields.Description:
Mihaela Rupnik Cek, a former Turistica student and now aspiring tourism entrepreneur, asked sTOUdio Turistica for help in creating the MORjE HAPPY KIDS website to promote the range of themed trails for families and animation programs for children at Slovenian Istria.

As part of the exercises in the subject Fundamentals of Computer Science, the students, under the guidance of Mag. Miha Bratec, carefully studied the modern guidelines and trends in the design of tourist websites and reviewed the technical solutions used in the test version of Mihaela's website. They suggested a number of improvements and enhancements in terms of functionality and usability to improve the user experience and increase the conversion rate of the website. The students also helped the entrepreneur improve the website's descriptions and structure visually and stylistically.

The collaboration proved to be extremely effective, as projects to create a website of one's own are often a major time and logistical feat for entrepreneurs, where students can be of great help with their digital skills and knowledge of trends in the digital world.
fields.Single line text: Mihaela Rupnik Cek, nekdanja študentka Turistice, je sTOUdio Turistica prosila za pomoč pri izdelavi spletne strani MORjE HAPPY KIDS za promocijo ponudbe tematskih poti za družine in animacijskih programov za otroke v Slovenski Istri.
fields.Single line ENG: sTOUdio Turistica was asked for help by Mihaela Rupnik Cek, a former Turistica student, in creating the MORjE HAPPY KIDS website to promote the range of themed trails for families and animation programs for children at Slovenian Istria.

fields.Description:
UP FTŠ Turistica student Anja Žagar Hrovatin solved the Slovenian Wine Queen challenge at the sTOUdio #WineFromZero 2020 weekend in collaboration with UP FM student Vanja Simeunović: "How to raise the profile of the Wine Queen during the COVID -19 crisis". As they presented good solutions to the given challenge, the 24th Wine Queen of Slovenia, Ana Pavlin, invited them to participate in the coordination of her business social networks. They were joined by other students from UP FTŠ Turistica and UP Faculty of Management, who will also do student internships as part of the project.

The Wine Queen team, led by Dr. Dejan Križaj, and the student Rudi Medved are helping to increase her visibility on social networks. Every week they create new content for her profiles and support Slovenian viticulture, Slovenian wine and Slovenian culture with innovative ideas. In this way, they gain useful experience in project management, analytics, writing, design, social media management and teamwork.
fields.Single line text: Anja Žagar Hrovatin z UP FTŠ Turistica in Vanja Simeunović z UP FM sta rešili izziv Slovenske vinske kraljice »Kako povečati prepoznavnost vinske kraljice v času krize COVID-19«, zato ju je 24. vinska kraljica Slovenije Ana Pavlin povabila k sodelovanju pri koordinaciji njenih poslovnih družbenih omrežjih.
fields.Single line ENG: Anja Žagar Hrovatin of UP FTŠ Turistica and Vanja Simeunović of UP FM solved the Slovenian Wine Queen challenge "How to raise the profile of the Wine Queen during the COVID-19 crisis", which is why the 24th Wine Queen of Slovenia, Ana Pavlin, invited them to participate in the coordination of her business social networks.

fields.Description:
The three-hour e-date was attended by 17 motivated and enthusiastic students from UP FTŠ Turistica from all over Slovenia (and also from abroad). As an overture, we went on an e-walk through Železniki under the guidance of the host of the date, the student Katja Kosem, and Mag. Špela Bertoncelj from TD Vigenjc, Železniki. We learned about the character and interesting tourist stories and products of this place.

After two "mining" presentations by students from other parts of Slovenia somehow connected to Železniki and its tradition of ore processing, the e-date took us to Serbia. Three Serbian students presented their places and their tourist impulse on UP FTŠ. All presentations affirm that even in difficult, non-mobile times we can be very active, connected, creative and productive.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo spletni sTOUdio zmenek, kjer smo se odpravili na e-pohod po Železnikih pod vodstvom gostiteljice zmenka študentke Katje Kosem in mag. Špela Bertoncelj iz TD Vigenjc, Železniki.
fields.Single line ENG: We held an online sTOUdio date where we went on an e-walk through Železniki under the guidance of the host of the date, the student Katja Kosem, and Mag. Špela Bertoncelj from TD Vigenjc, Železniki.

fields.Description:
Under the mentorship of sTOUdio mentor dr. Tina Orel Frank, students edited, proofread and translated website texts from Slovenian into English. We invited smaller local tourism businesses to join sTOUdio and translated over 7,000 words and proofread over 26,000.

The students were grateful to have had the opportunity to learn English by working in real-life situations and the skills they gained will help them in their future careers. The organizations and individuals involved really appreciated this form of collaboration: the Tourist Cooperative Zakladi Istre, Mateja Kregar Gliha s.p., Hisa Robida and #TourSlove.
fields.Single line text: Pod mentorstvom sTOUdio mentorice dr. Tine Orel Frank in v sodelovanju z manjšimi lokalnimi turističnimi podjetji so študenti uredili, lektorirali in prevedli besedila spletnih strani iz slovenščine v angleščino.
fields.Single line ENG: Under the mentorship of sTOUdio mentor dr. Tina Orel Frank and in collaboration with small local tourism businesses, students edited, proofread and translated website texts from Slovenian into English.

fields.Description:
The fourth sTOUdio DATE or. the 2nd sTOUdio e-DATE happened online and it took place on Friday, 24.04.2020 in Marezige at the viewpoint of Wine Fountain. A very constructive meeting was held with 13 prospective students, the director of the Wine Fountain Alen Babič, mentor dr. Dejan Križaj and landscape architect Ms Romana Kačič.

By way of introduction, we heard Alen Babić introduce Marezige and the Wine Fountain to the participants. This was followed by the students of the 3rd year of the program UNI at the Faculty UP Turistica and the main organizers of this event; Jaka Godejša, Marcel Višner and Ajda Medvešek, with the presentation of the idea of a photo point that would complement the existing offer of Marezige and Wine Fountain respectively. Landscape architect Ms Romana Kačič, professionally and pictorially argued the presented idea. She gave those present a set of guidelines that must generally be taken into account when placing tourist products in the space and steered the idea group on the right path.

The meeting continued with suggestions, comments and observations from the other participants. A very productive meeting ended with suggestions and wishes for further collaboration and networking between various experts and students.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo spletni sTOUdio zmenek z direktorjem Vinske fontane v Marezigah Alenom Babičem in krajinsko arhitektko Romano Kačič, skupaj z mentorjem dr. Dejanom Križajem in 13 našimi študenti, z vizijo postavitve foto točke pri Vinski fontani.
fields.Single line ENG: We held an online sTOUdio date with the director of the Wine Fountain in Marezige Alen Babič and landscape architect Ms Romana Kačič, together with our mentor dr. Dejan Križaj and 13 of our students, with the vision of erecting a photo point at the Wine Fountain.

fields.Description:
For the travel agency TJs and Travel Roundabout https://www.travelroundabout.com, the students conducted three online surveys and prepared detailed reports that were presented to the client:

- Research and definition of boutique accommodation in different regions (quality analysis and definition of the best).

- Research and definition of tourist farms to stay or engage in (quality analysis and definition of the best).

- Research and definition of outdoor activities in Slovenia and Croatia that could be the basis for new 5* experiences.
fields.Single line text: Potovalna agencija TJs in Travel Roundabout je našim študentom postavila izziv. Izvedli so tri spletne ankete in pripravili podrobna poročila, ki so jih predstavili naročniku.
fields.Activity: Roundabout - analysis of the offer and 5* experiences
fields.Single line ENG: Travel agency TJs and Travel Roundabout gave our students a challenge. They conducted three online surveys and prepared detailed reports that were presented to the client.

fields.Description:
#MyStoryFromZero is a campaign on the social networks of the initiative https://www.localsfromzero.org/ with the aim to organically increase their follower base and spread interesting travel stories.

The stories describe interesting incidents that happened due to unfamiliarity with the culture, language or describe the local peculiarities of the countries that surprised the travelers during their trips. So #MyStoryFromZero are not ordinary travel stories, but stories that reflect the peculiarities of the local environment to which the storytellers have traveled.

In the first two months of 2021, 10 students and travelers from all over Slovenia have shared their stories on LocalsFromZero profiles. They have happened in countries all over the world: from Thailand, Indonesia, Georgia, Australia to Spain, Portugal, Ireland and Scotland; describing everything from sleeping on the pier, playing with local kids to meeting a giant kangaroo.
fields.Single line text: #MyStoryFromZero je kampanja na družbenih omrežjih iniciative Locals From Zero z namenom organskega povečevanja baze sledilcev in širjenja zanimivih popotniških zgodb.
fields.Single line ENG: #MyStoryFromZero is a campaign on the social networks of the initiative Locals From Zero with the aim to organically increase their follower base and spread interesting travel stories.

fields.Description:
On Sunday, March 28, 2021, the first live sTOUdio DATE after a long time took place in Dob near Domžale. A group of 5 students of UP FTŠ Turistica visited Franci Kek in accordance with all applicable measures. He made us laugh as soon as we arrived and the mood only increased until the end of our three-hour meeting.

We not only laughed at his jokes and adventures, but were impressed by his enthusiasm as he told us about the 1797 house and its apartments. He told us stories of famous historical figures, as well as the whole house having a strong historical feel. Add to that Franci's humorous story and the experience is guaranteed. The discussion then moved on to other collaborations and marketing opportunities for houses and experiences. In addition to the cold cuts, anecdotes about the organization of Rock Otočec and the recording of Kek's hidden cameras were touched upon. 

Determined to work together in the future, we left Domžale yesterday with big smiles under our masks - because we saw each other live again after a long time and, of course, because we discovered a huge tourist potential that day.
fields.Single line text: Skupina 5 študentov UP FTŠ Turistice je imela sTOUdio zmenek s Francijem Kekom, ki so ga obiskali v Dobu pri Domžalah. Razkazal jim je hišo iz leta 1797 in pripadajoča stanovanja ter jim pripovedoval o bogati zgodovini hiše.
fields.Single line ENG: A group of 5 students of UP FTŠ Turistica had a sTOUdio date with Franci Kek, whom they visited in Dob near Domžale. He showed them the 1797 house and its apartments and told them about the rich history of the house.

fields.Description:
For the first time in 2021, enthusiastic students from UP FTŠ Turistica and our host Franjo Penko, the owner of Turn Castle in Ilirska Bistrica, met for a sTOUdio session held online. After the introductory welcome and presentations by Dr. Križaj and the owner of the castle, the director of TIC Ilirska Bistrica , Mrs. Maja Uljan, presented the history of the nearby Prem Castle and the possibilities of cooperation and connection with the castle. Then the students took over. With the help of student Neža's video, we virtually walked around the castle and discussed examples of inspiring practices for managing similar institutions or estates prepared by Ula and Tajda. This was followed by a discussion of the possibilities and aspirations of the owner and his family, all of which are intensely embedded in the history of the mansion.

After two hours of socializing, we said goodbye with a promise to continue working together and, above all, to repeat the appointment live as soon as the situation allows.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo spletni sTOUdio zmenek s Franjem Penkom, ki nam je predstavil grad Turn v Ilirski Bistrici, katerega lastnik je, in z Majo Uljan, direktorico TIC Ilirska Bistrica, ki nam je predstavila bližnji grad Prem.
fields.Single line ENG: We held an online sTOUdio date with Franjo Penko, who presented us with the Turn Castle in Ilirska Bistrica which he owns, and with Maja Uljan, director of TIC Ilirska Bistrica, who presented us with the nearby Prem Castle.

fields.Description:
On May 4, 2021, we gathered technology and tourism enthusiasts - we considered how modern technologies such as Blockchain and NFT will affect the development of the tourism industry.

After the presentation of the basics of Blockchain technology, presented to us by a young expert in this field, Denis Markun, our sTOUdio colleagues Jaka Godejša and Didi Divja Volk, students from UP FTŠ Turistica, presented examples and ways of implementing such technologies in tourism.

The intense debate lasted two and a half hours and was followed by 22 participants. We ended the meeting with the promise that it will be necessary to organize another unifying event on this topic, as the essence of tourism is precisely the connection of several stakeholders in a common, technologically appropriate offer.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek, kjer smo zbrali ljubitelje tehnologije in turizma. Razmišljali smo, kako bodo sodobne tehnologije, kot sta Blockchain in NFT, vplivale na razvoj turistične industrije.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio e-DATE - Tehnologije prihodnosti v turizmu
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date where we gathered technology and tourism enthusiasts. We considered how modern technologies such as Blockchain and NFT will affect the development of the tourism industry.

fields.Description:
On May 15, 2021, the much-awaited sTOUdio e- date began at 5pm: "Young People for the Future of Tourism - the Vision and Role of Young Professionals in Tourism Today and Tomorrow".

In our midst, we had four young, sincere and optimistic guys as guests, each of whom is a success story in his field in the hotel and tourism industry. The leaseholder of the popular Napoleon bar in Bela krajina - Alen Šišić, the co-owner of the restaurant and accommodation Kozjanski dvor - Sandi Štus, the owner of the experience restaurant COB - Filip Matjaž and the coordinator of the initiative Localsfromzero - Rudi Medved shared their success stories with us, including all the ups and downs .

We also talked about the problems and business opportunities brought by the pandemic - the passion and love for work and their optimistic view of the future radiated to all present.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek na temo Vizija in vloga mladih v turizmu danes in jutri s štirimi mladimi in optimističnimi gosti s področja hotelirstva in turizma.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio e-DATE - Young people for the future of tourism
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date on the topic of the Vision and Role of Young Professionals in Tourism Today and Tomorrow with four young and optimistic guests from the field of the hotel and tourism industry.

fields.Description:
In the Rižana valley there is a place that the locals call "Pri Bobrih" (by the beavers), where a new tourist attraction is being built.

On Friday 9 July 2021, a group of seven students from Turistica attended a workshop where Mr Valter Krmac (owner of the attraction) took us through the project plans.

He showed us the entire site where the new innovative tourist experience will be built in the future. It will combine culinary and accommodation services. The inspiration will come from the local environment and the historical event of the Placitum of Riziano. 

The owner also showed us the neighbouring trout farm with which he will collaborate in the future.

We were very impressed with the determination and concept as it will revitalise the place and put it on the tourist map.

The student group will continue the collaboration under the guidance of sTOUdio Turistica. The aim of the students is to put professional knowledge into practise and plan unforgettable experiences for the future guests of the attraction.
fields.Single line text: Skupina študentov Turistice se je udeležila delavnice, kjer nas je g. Valter Krmac, lastnik nove turistične atrakcije »Pri Bobrih« v Rižanski dolini, popeljal skozi projektne načrte.
fields.Activity: Designing experiences for the emerging tourist attraction "Pri Bobrih" (Rižana)
fields.Single line ENG: A group of students from Turistica attended a workshop where Mr Valter Krmac, owner of the new tourist attraction "Pri Bobrih" (at the beavers') in Rižana valley, took us through the project plans.

fields.Description:
On Saturday, August 7, 2021, we met in Smokvica, where our sTOUdio trip took place in the hot afternoon. Later, it developed into an in-depth sTOUdio debate date. First, David Babič said a few introductory words and introduced us to his hometown. Then his brother Erik Babič joined us. He showed us the yard of the Bržan homestead and told us some interesting things about the homestead and Smokvica.

Afterward, we took a walk through the village, stopping on the way at some special places, such as the old traditional chimney, the fig growing out of the wall, the plantation with new figs... On the walk, we talked with David and Gianni about the event "Mladost in Smokvica" and how the "Šagra" used to be. ("Šagra" = a traditional celebration that takes place on the feast day of a saint. The church in Smokvica is dedicated to Mary Magdalene and is celebrated on July 22).

We returned to the homestead, where we deepened the topics that were on the agenda. We shared our expertise in tourism and experience design, social work, and psychology. It all blended together very well into a good conversation where everyone learned something new. We realized that Smokvica has great potential for developing unique experiences.

We all agreed to work together and stay in touch. The Babič brothers then kindly invited us to dinner, which was followed by a cozy get-together into the evening.
fields.Single line text: Na tokratnem sTOUdio izletu smo obiskali Smokvico in izkusili njeno pestro ponudbo. Videli smo, da ima to gručasto naselje veliko potenciala za razvoj edinstvenih izkušenj.
fields.Single line ENG: On this sTOUdio trip we visited Smokvica and experienced its many offerings. We see this clustered settlement as having a lot of potential for developing unique experiences.

fields.Description:
Once again, we left the event inspired and full of new ideas! 💡

The theme of the international event we held a few days ago as part of the 29th Nordic Symposium in Iceland was the role of young tourism and innovation professionals in tomorrow’s global tourism. The event was moderated by two students, Anja from the University of Primorska, Faculty of Tourism Studies - Turistica and Sabrina from Munich University of Applied Sciences, and they were joined by 4 young, enthusiastic and ambitious female entrepreneurs who shared many stories from their personal and professional lives.

Isabel, Tina, Philomena and Moana have already gained a wealth of entrepreneurial experience at a young age, which they are happy to share.

You certainly should not be short of inspiration after an event like this! How fortunate that this was one of the many international events our team will be organising in the future. Keep following us and see you next time. 🙌

More information about the event: http://www.airth.global/depositview.aspx?dpid=10001&lng=en
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo mednarodni dogodek v okviru 29. Nordijskega simpozija na Islandiji, ki se je osredotočal na vlogo mladih turističnih in inovacijskih strokovnjakov v globalnem turizmu prihodnosti.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio e-Date - Role of young tourism and innovation professionals in tomorrow’s global tourism

Attachments.filename: Role of young tourism and innovation professionals in tomorrow's global tourism (6).png

fields.Single line ENG: We held an international event as part of the 29th Nordic Symposium in Iceland focused on the role of young tourism and innovation professionals in tomorrow’s global tourism.

fields.Description: We have found that the Slovenian region of Koroška is truly a treasure full of surprises, as its slogan says! It is one of the smallest regions in Slovenia, but it definitely offers a whole range of interesting things - from natural beauty, rich heritage, historical sights to tasty dishes. Although it was quite a busy day and we experienced a lot, it was definitely not our last visit to this amazing region.
fields.Single line text: Na tokratnem sTOUdio izletu smo obiskali Koroško in izkusili številne nepozabne posebnosti te regije.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio TRIP Koroška - A treasure full of suprises
fields.Single line ENG: On this sTOUdio trip we visited Koroška and experienced the region's many unforgettable quirks.

fields.Description:
Whenever we can, we accept our partners' invitations to visit good tourism practices in Slovenia (and beyond). This time we visited some of the gems of the Pomurje region.
On Wednesday 24 November, we met with the students and partners of UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica at the EXPANO adventure park, where the manager, Helga Lukač, presented how the facility came from Milan (Expo) to Pomurje, where since 2019 it has been a gateway to the wider offer and heritage of Pomurje.

After visiting EXPANO, we went to nearby Lendava - to the Vinarium Tower, where we were introduced to the project of a scattered hotel in the Lendava hills in the immediate vicinity.

We ended our excursion at the Vidov Brejg herb garden in Goričko, where again there was no shortage of local delicacies and we also learned about the enviable effects of successful networking of local providers!

The incredible potential of tourism in Pomurje, the enthusiastic and dedicated providers and the excellent cuisine. These, we can say, are the main highlights of our trip. We will visit the Bevog brewery, which we also wanted to visit, next time as due to Austria's lockdown.
fields.Single line text: Na tokratnem sTOUdio izletu smo si ogledali nekaj dobrih turističnih praks Pomurja.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio TRIP - Gems of the Pomurje region
fields.Single line ENG: On this sTOUdio trip we visited some of the good tourism practices of the Pomurje region.

fields.Description: After a long time we gathered live at UP FTŠ Turistica, where a new sTOUdio DATE took place. The guest was the expert in storytelling, Vojka Žgavec Clemenz. The event was extremely informative, interesting and full of good energy! Vojka made the date even more interactive with a competition where participants learned more about the elements of storytelling. We're all looking forward to new collaborations! And as Vojka says, "Have dreams and put them into words."
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek z Vojko Žgavec Clemenz, strokovnjakinjo za pripovedovanje, ki nas je skozi zabavno tekmovanje poučila o prvinah pripovedovanja.
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date with Vojka Žgavec Clemenz, an expert in storytelling, who taught us about the elements of storytelling through a fun competition.

fields.Description:
On Friday, February 25, 2022, we held a sTOUdio TRIP entitled "Invisible Lublana", where we participated in a guided tour where we got to know Ljubljana through the eyes of the homeless. We were guided by Gašper who also experienced very well what it's like to have no roof over your head and to be at the mercy of nature's will. He guided us through the capital while telling his life story and showed us the places where Ljubljana's homeless usually gather, where they can afford a hot meal, where they can get dry for a few moments and where they can spend the night.

First-grader Tjaša described her experience as follows:
"During his tour, he told us about his life with a tremendous will to live and a glow in his eyes that I'll never forget. Nevertheless, his listening made my heart pound several times and shook me to the core. After only an hour of walking, I felt the power of nature, as the heavy rain and cold almost drove me out of my mind and I could hardly wait to go somewhere warm. I can't imagine how hard it's for homeless people right now. Wind and sub-zero temperatures make you pretty tired, and when you think about it, reaching for drugs doesn't seem so incomprehensible anymore. I'll definitely remember the trip and from now on I'll also see Ljubljana with different eyes. "
fields.Single line text: Na tokratnem sTOUdio izletu smo se udeležili vodenega ogleda Ljubljane skozi oči brezdomcev.
fields.Single line ENG: On this sTOUdio trip we participated in a guided tour where we got to know Ljubljana through the eyes of the homeless.

fields.Description:
Touristology is a one-of-a-kind newsletter project that was created by our students and its mission is an exploration of human leisure activities on & off the Internet. It is written by tourism students from around the world and it is published every two weeks. It focuses on topics such as technology, experiences, culture, marketing, work, productivity and leisure, remote work,  innovations, Web 3.0 …all in the context of tourism.
Toruistology newsletter is intended for all tourism professionals, businesses and students who want to stay up to date on trends in the tourism industry and gain insights into Generation Z, which is creating this newsletter. 
The goal is to make it easy for students and other professionals to be informed about innovations in the tourism industry and gain a broader look at the industry.
The newsletter is an open project, so anyone that is a member of sTOUdio Discord community can contribute to the content of the newsletter, give tips and suggestions for its improvement, and anyone interested in writing, coordinating or designing the newsletter is also welcome to become a member of the team behind the newsletter.

Read past issues - https://us20.campaign-archive.com/home/?u=7364e4b29f50a57b7926ca4c9&id=8f1af2d632
fields.Single line text: Touristology je edinstveno glasilo, ki so ga ustvarili naši študenti in čigar poslanstvo je raziskovanje človekovih prostočasnih dejavnosti na in izven interneta.
fields.Single line ENG: Touristology is a unique newsletter created by our students whose mission is to explore human leisure activities on and off the Internet.

fields.Description:
Besides a wonderful trip, our students Tjaša and Anita, challengers from the business world, presented Vojka Žgavec Clemenz with their solutions on how to convince potential buyers of Teambuilding and its other products in times of crisis. Vojka's offer to our students to manage their business TikTok profile also shows the success of the event.
Well, of course, there was no lack of karst delicacies on the trip, provided by Herbal Farm Rogelja and Tourist Farm Leban, where we also experienced the traditional osmica. Many thanks also to the Miren kras Tourist Board for the excellent presentation of their activities!
See you on the next trip!
fields.Single line text: Odpeljali smo se na Kras, kjer sta naši študentki Tjaša in Anita predstavili svoje rešitve poslovnega izziva Vojke Žgavec Clemez. Na izletu ni manjkalo kraških dobrot, doživeli pa smo tudi tradicionalno osmico.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio TRIP - Karra Team Building Karst
fields.Single line ENG: We took a trip to Karst, where our students Tjaša and Anita presented their solutions to a business challenge from Vojka Žgavec Clemez. There was no lack of karst delicacies on the trip and we also experienced the traditional osmica.

fields.Description:
On Thursday, Andrej P. Škraba took us deeper into the world of Bitcoin ₿ and Blockchain technologies. On the most numerous sTOUdio date to date, he presented what specific problems Bitcoin solves, how it came into existence and what its potential applications are. 

This event was the first of the events we will organise at sTOUdio on cryptocurrencies and blockchain technologies. The next ones will focus even more on concrete applications of these technologies in tourism.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek z Andrejem P. Škrabo, ki nas je popeljal globlje v svet Bitcoina ₿ in Blockchain tehnologij.
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date with Andrej P. Škraba, who took us deeper into the world of Bitcoin ₿ and Blockchain technologies.

fields.Description:
On Friday, April 1, 2022, part of the sTOUdio team travelled to the Karst region, where we presented our solutions to Vojka Žgavec Clemenz, storytelling expert and co-founder of two of her own brands, Mmm Beatrice and Karra. Vojka loves Karst and her work combines decades of experience in traditional and digital marketing with her personal experience in brand management. 

We were honoured to introduce her and help her solve the challenge she had given us at a previous date at sTOUdio. So, we solved the problem of how to attract companies to participate in team building programmes in the Karst region in a crisis situation. We presented our ideas and views, divided the work and started researching the teambuilding market, why we organise teambuilding, the trends for this year and the competition. We found that teambuilding is not a cost, but an investment in the employees and the company, and we found interesting examples of products in tourism. We identified customers and did a market analysis and then gave it our suggestions for improvement. We focused on our social media content and how to engage customers with different marketing strategies and become more digital. We also gave her ideas ''from zero'' on how to improve her tourism experience. 

After a pleasant meeting, we also had an interesting debate that was very educational and entertaining.
fields.Single line text: Del ekipe sTOUdia se je odpravil na Kras, da bi Vojki Žgavec Clemenz, strokovnjakinji na področju storytellinga in soustanoviteljici dveh lastnih blagovnih znamk Mmm Beatrice in Karra, predstavil svoje rešitve na vprašanje kako povečati povpraševanje po teambuildingih na Krasu.
fields.Activity: Karra Challenge - how to raise demand for team-buildings in the Karst region
fields.Single line ENG: Part of the sTOUdio team travelled to Karst to present their solutions on how to raise demand for team-buildings in the region to Vojka Žgavec Clemenz, storytelling expert and co-founder of two of her own brands, Mmm Beatrice and Karra.

fields.Description:
Vuly Nazim is a PhD student at the Faculty of tourism studies - Turistica. She comes from the Maldives but lives in Slovenia with her husband and 2-year-old son.

During the event she presented two topics:

1) Her PhD research - which focuses on Atmospherics in Hospitality and their impact on guest behavior.

2) Her hotel business, which she runs from Slovenia. She owns a guesthouse with 15 rooms in the Maldives called h78
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek z Vuly Nazim, doktorsko študentko na Fakulteti za turistične študije - Turistica, ki je spregovorila o svoji doktorski raziskavi, osredotočeni na vzdušje v gostinstvu in o njenem hotelskem poslu na Maldivih, od koder je doma.
fields.Activity: UP FTŠ sTOUdio e-date: Turistica PhD student Vuly Nazim - hotelier from Maldives
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date with Vuly Nazim, a PhD student at the Faculty of tourism studies - Turistica, who talked of her PhD research on Atmospherics in Hospitality and her hotel business in the Maldives, where she is originally from.

fields.Description:
UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica hosted an online conversation with Klemen Furlan, CEO of GoGiro, a tourism start-up that stands out for its innovative solutions for mobility in tourism. It is characterized by two main pillars:

* An app that offers sustainable and digitally supported experiences and encourages exploration of the area surrounding hotels.

* E-scooters and e-bikes that provide hotel guests with interesting, fun and efficient sustainable mobility.

With this event we introduced new type of category: sTOUdio TALK.

sTOUdio TALK is a lecture event for students of UP FTŠ Turistica on current tourism development trends and innovations, to which we invite guest speakers and the general public. This type of event is introduced and encouraged because we strive to share knowledge and create opportunities for networking and discourse among all tourism stakeholders.
fields.Single line text: UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica je gostil spletni pogovor s Klemnom Furlanom, direktorjem GoGiro, turističnega start-upa, ki izstopa po inovativnih rešitvah za mobilnost v turizmu.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio TALK: Klemen Furlan - GoGiro startup
fields.Single line ENG: UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica hosted an online conversation with Klemen Furlan, CEO of GoGiro, a tourism start-up that stands out for its innovative solutions for mobility in tourism.

fields.Description:
In May, the teams from UP FTS sTOUdio Turistica and AIRTH prepared a panel that shook the listener’s view on UNESCO heritage. We asked ourselves “Can contemporary lifestyles qualify for the UNESCO World Heritage status’’?

We hosted three amazing organizations and individuals that are working hard to gain the UNESCO World Heritage status to protect what they are passionate about. We hosted:

- An amazing Egyptian Belly dancer, Amie Sultan, that is professionally educating Egyptian women in the art of Egyptian dance, with the mission to move belly dance from basements to theaters, where it belongs.
- A passionate espresso representative, which taught us that the Nespresso machine for making espresso is problematic only when it doesn’t produce foam. In this case, we have a problem with the grinder.
- And two driven representatives of Berlin techno music that talked about how important is community in the UNESCO application process, and that the most important thing isn’t who invented the culture, but where the culture is thriving. 

We would again like to thank all the panelists for their passionate discussion moderated by Rudi Medved and Mladen Mitrović.
fields.Single line text: Dogodek, ki so ga študentje organizirali v okviru UNESCOVE konference v Portorožu. Na online okrogli mizi so gostili zanimive goste iz Egipta,  Italije in Berlina.
fields.Single line ENG: An event organised by students in the framework of the UNESCO conference in Portorož. They hosted interesting guests from Egypt, Italy and Berlin for an online round table.

fields.Description:
On Monday, May 9th, Turistica hosted an event with Martin Petrič, a visual communications designer and innovator, who took us on a journey of discovering the many dimensions of NFT applications in tourism and culture. This event was the second in a row on the topic of web3 and crypto!

Martin shared with us some of his own ventures in the NFT art space, as well as the difference between good and bad practices. He explained the creative process behind launching NFTs and its possible implications for society. He raised awareness about the power this technology holds and about the dangers of an unregulated market - new projects involving blockchain should always have a purpose greater than just “let's do it because it’s the cool new thing”. 

NFTs should add value, not spoil it - and much of that value is lost because of the lack of collaboration between designers, developers, and marketers.
fields.Single line text: Izvedli smo sTOUdio zmenek z Martinom Petričem, oblikovalcem vizualnih komunikacij in inovatorjem, ki nas je popeljal na pot odkrivanja številnih dimenzij NFT aplikacij v turizmu in kulturi.
fields.Single line ENG: We held a sTOUdio date with Martin Petrič, a visual communications designer and innovator, who took us on a journey of discovering the many dimensions of NFT applications in tourism and culture.

fields.Description: During the project, in which we cooperated with Treasures of Istria, participants Tjaša Hajnžič, Tilen Nipič, Tjaša Pristovšek and mentor Jaka Godejša acquired many new skills in writing marketing texts for tourist experiences. The texts of selected experiences listed on the website https://www.zakladi-istre.si/ were re-written and/or translated into English. We learned how to edit texts to make them more attractive to tourists and what is the most important information a tourist needs to know in order to decide to buy the selected product. We were all happy to have helped and satisfied with the final product that was created with our help. We were also rewarded. We received training (3-hour workshop) on copywriting. But that's not all. Treasures of Istria took us on a trip to the herbal paradise of Slovenian Istria. This is located in the village of Trsek, which belongs to the municipality of Koper and the municipality of Marezige. The Posedi couple took us up to 298 meters above sea level and showed us their mountain farm. It was very interesting, especially because they produce everything naturally with a modern steam distillation plant, whose process we could see with our own eyes. We loved that they want to bring people closer to natural products - this is a great mission, because as they said, everything we need for a healthy life comes from nature, which we must respect a lot! They also keep bees in the herbal paradise of Slovenian Istria and have set up an Istrian-style beehive in 2022, listed on UNESCO. In addition, they offer a herbal energy trail for tourists. After tasting and buying useful and environmentally friendly souvenirs, the excursion was not over yet. After lunch at the nearby Karjola restaurant, we discussed the project with the director of Treasures of Istria, and after successful cooperation, we took lovely picture together with an ice cream in hand at the photo spot in Marezige.
fields.Single line text: Študentje UP FTŠ sTOUdia Turistice so se ob sodelovanju z Zakladi Istre učili pisanja besedil, kjer so osvojili številne spretnosti pri pisanju in oblikovanju marketinških vsebin za turistična doživetja.
fields.Single line ENG: The students of UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica, in cooperation with Treasures of Istria, learned of copywriting, where they acquired many skills in writing and designing marketing content for tourist experiences.

fields.Description:
Together with the restaurant EDEN in the garden of the Kržišnik Gallery in Žiri, sTOUdio students and mentors organised the first dinner in Slovenia, where guests made a reservation by purchasing an NFT.
 
The wave of enthusiasm for the combination of hospitality and new technologies is confirmed by the fact that the 17-course dinner... was sold out. Guests paid a total of 1 Ether for the 10 tickets available (at the time of writing, that's just under €1,600).
 
The successful project represents one of the first important Slovenian and international steps in exploring the possibilities offered by new crypto technologies such as NFT for the hospitality industry.
 
During the course of the project, several more protocols and transactions were implemented and tested with dinner participants, which will serve as excellent examples of best practises for all partners involved, both in developing new similar products and in educating all future early adopters of the exciting, useful (and of course, as always, potentially dangerous) innovations that the crypto world brings to the hospitality industry.
 
The founders of the project:
- Tadej Kržišnik, chef, EDEN restaurant, krzisnik.eu
- Boris Balant, designer
- Jaka Godejša, co-founder of FromZero Travelers, student at UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica
 
Project partners:
- UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica
- FromZero Alliance
- Rajzefiber PAKT Incubator and Centre for Creativity

Video: Matevž Zupanc
fields.Single line text: Študenti in mentorji sTOUdia smo skupaj z restavracijo EDEN na vrtu Galerije Kržišnik v Žireh organizirali prvo večerjo v Sloveniji, kjer so gostje opravili rezervacijo z nakupom NFT.
fields.Single line ENG: sTOUdio students and mentors, together with the restaurant EDEN in the garden of the Kržišnik Gallery in Žiri, organised the first dinner in Slovenia, where guests made a reservation by purchasing an NFT.

fields.Description: Matjaž walked us through the journey of his work as a photographer after graduating from Turistica. He talked about his favorite projects and showed a lot of his work. Each photograph had a unique story, some were fun and others educational. He showed us portraits of famous personalities he had photographed, and projects like Movie Industry in China, Punk in Myanmar, Mars on Earth, Surfing China, Tattooed Shanghai and others
fields.Single line text: UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica je gostila sTOUdio TALK s fotografom Matjažem Tančičem. Predstavil je svojo pot po končanem študiju na Turistici, življenju na Kitajskem in fotografiranju marsičesa zanimivega, od modelov do vsakdanjih ljudi, pokrajin in dogodkov.
fields.Single line ENG: UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica hosted a sTOUdio TALK with photographer Matjaž Tančič. He talked of his journey after graduating from Turistica, living in China and photographing many interesting scenes, from models to everyday people, landscapes and events.

fields.Description:
On Friday, January 20, 2023, we had as our guest the newly elected President of the Cooperative Zakladi Istre Robi Marsič. He gave the students an overview of the cooperative's activities, presented the challenges faced by rural tourism providers, and spoke about his own observations regarding the development of tourism in his village of Popetre. He emphasized the importance of collaboration, a change in the mindset of locals who need to recognize the value of their environment, and the importance of bottom-up approaches. These can be very small actions, such as putting benches so people can sit down and enjoy nature, etc. During the session, we also recognized many opportunities for using digital tools, for example, we could use the podcast equipment of the sTOUdio room to record and preserve the intangible cultural heritage of the elderly, as many stories, traditions and knowledge are lost.

The event was organized as an introductory activity for the students participating in the "PKP Let's Digitalize Periphery" project led by teaching assistant Tadej Rogelja.
fields.Single line text: Robi Marsič, novoizvoljeni predsednik zadruge Zakladi Istre, je razpravljal o izzivih s katerimi se soočajo ponudniki turizma na podeželju ter pomenu sodelovanja, spremembe mišljenja in pristopov od spodaj navzgor.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio DATE - Challenges of rural tourism entrepreneurship with Robi Marsič
fields.Single line ENG: Robi Marsič, the newly elected President of the Cooperative Zakladi Istre, discussed the challenges faced by rural tourism providers and the importance of collaboration, a change in mindset, and bottom-up approaches at the sTOUdio DATE event.

fields.Description:
On the 5th of April we held an exciting discussion about the future of Web3 in tourism and events! A big shoutout to our experts Addy Creeze and Maksim Izmaylov, as well as moderator Dr James Morgan. 

Missed out? Check out the recording at the UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D_c0iuySMtQ

The event was organized in collaboration between UP FTŠ sTOUdio Turistica and the University of Westminster.
fields.Single line text: V sodelovanju z Univerzo Westminster iz Londona smo organizirali online panel o Internetu 3.0 (web3) in njegovemu vplivu na turizem in dogodke.
fields.Activity: sTOUdio TALK: Web3, Tourism and Events

Attachments.filename: Web 3 in T&E - Banner (1000 × 500, pik) (1) WEbsiter.png
sTOUdio Talk Web3 Tourism and Events website 2.jpg

fields.Single line ENG: In cooperation with the University of Westminster, London, we organised an online panel on Web3 and its impact on tourism and events.

fields.Description:
On Tuesday, 25 April 2023, we successfully held an sTOUdio date with Domn Kert in the sTOUdio room on the topic of growth marketing and coworking spaces. The event took place between 16:00 and 17:30 and was attended by 6 students and young entrepreneurs who pre-registered for the event.

Domen Kert (https://www.linkedin.com/in/domenkert/), an experienced growth marketer and owner of the coworking space Firma in Maribor, shared his extensive experience and knowledge in the field of growth marketing and presented his journey as a founder of the startup Travelicious and a member of the prestigious YCombinator.

Participants had the opportunity to ask questions to Domen, which led to interesting discussions about the challenges of growth marketing, best practices for business growth and trends and the future of coworking spaces. Domen shared valuable advice and examples from his practice and encouraged participants to think about how they can apply the knowledge they have gained in their own businesses or careers.

The discussion was followed by informal socialising and networking among the participants, who had the opportunity to meet Domen in person, network with other students and entrepreneurs and exchange experiences and ideas.
fields.Single line text: Na sTOUdio zmenku z Domnom Kertom smo raziskovali svet growth marketinga in coworking prostorov, kjer so udeleženci pridobili dragocene vpoglede in nasvete za svoje podjetniške poti.
fields.Single line ENG: On the sTOUdio date with Domn Kert, we explored the world of growth marketing and coworking spaces, where participants gained valuable insights and advice for their entrepreneurial journeys.

fields.Description:
Living Learning Labs focus on revitalizing rural and peripheral destinations with the help of local youth and responsible travelers.

We believe that leisure and vacation should be treated and experienced as sacred time.

To achieve this, we work with locals and visitors to organically inspire non-staged hospitality, intrinsic innovation and sincere sustainability.

The concept of Living Learning Labs won Hack Startup Village 2022 competition. 

First Living Learning Lab happened in a small Karst village Temnica from 24th - 29th of June 2023. You can read more about the pilot version here: https://mirror.xyz/0xa35299dd9FFA87Cc0D3C4f5f61295FD47834076b/Cw3mdIBdBVq6iLLC1cKultudjyavOyQ2UGTUCCgACBk

Video recap: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=lzHkTfaANFE
fields.Single line text: Living Learning Labi se osredotočajo na oživljanje podeželskih in perifernih destinacij s pomočjo lokalne mladine in odgovornih popotnikov.
fields.Single line ENG: Living Learning Labs focus on revitalizing rural and peripheral destinations with the help of local youth and responsible travelers.

fields.Description:
The students, accompanied by their pedagogical mentor Tade Rogelj, embarked on an enriching journey to explore the offerings of the Treasures of Istria Cooperative. Their first stop was the Marima Farm, nestled in the quaint village of Trebeše, which is home to only about 25 residents. Led by the farm's owner, Urška Klančar, the group ventured to the highest waterfall in Slovenian Istria, known as the Stranice waterfall or the Sladki slap. They then proceeded to explore the terraced organic olive orchard, where manual cultivation is a necessity due to the steep 43% slope. Along the way, the students discovered the traditional ways of life and agricultural practices of the local inhabitants. The farm's specialty, organic olive oil and dried fruit, was savored and relished by all.

After a pleasant stroll through the village of Trebeše, the students embarked on the next leg of their excursion, arriving at the Ražman Wine Cellar in the village of Gračišče. Rok Ražman warmly welcomed them and introduced them to the intricacies of wine production while they indulged in the tasting of three exquisite wines. This visit also shed light on the invaluable benefits of networking and collaboration among different providers within the cooperative.

The final destination was Kantina Romano, owned by Robi Maršič, who also served as the project mentor. Here, the students were treated to a delectable feast of homemade culinary delights, creating a delightful atmosphere for socializing and engaging in lively discussions. As the event drew to a close, a public presentation was held to showcase the project's outcomes. The students proudly presented their accomplishments in tackling digital challenges, thereby contributing to the growth of local businesses and enhancing the visibility of the micro providers they had chosen to support.
fields.Single line text: Skupina študentov se je v okviru projekta "Digitalizirajmo periferijo podala na terenski obisk v Slovensko Istro, da bi bolje razumela izzive in poslovanje mikro ponudnikov na podeželju!
fields.Single line ENG: A group of students went on a field visit to Slovenian Istria in the framework of the project "Digitising the Periphery" to better understand the challenges and business of micro providers in rural areas!

fields.Description:
As the Digitalising the Periphery PKP project draws to a close, the students have prepared a second public presentation of the results. It took place on Friday, 26 May in the sTOUdio room. During the event, the students summarised the entire project process, from the initial stages of getting to know the working mentor, the Cooperative Treasures of Istria, in January, to the interviewing of potential providers in February, to the practical work with providers in March and April, to the field visit to the micro-providers of Slovenian Istria in May. 

The focus was on presenting the digital challenges solved, which was the very purpose of the project. A number of solutions were presented that helped the selected micro providers to become visible not only in the local environment but also online, thus attracting a wider public. 

The students created several new websites, made interesting promotional videos, created and managed Facebook and Instagram profiles, wrote useful guides on how to use a POS terminal, how to set up and manage a website, how to use Canva to create promotional materials and published guidelines for successful digital marketing. They also worked on new experiences that the selected micro-vendors have/will integrate into their offer to attract new segments of guests. Finally, they summarised their cooperation with the providers and presented the new skills and knowledge they have acquired during the project and the challenges they have faced during the project. 

For those of you who were not able to attend the event live, please watch the recording: https://fb.watch/kWZk1mI3tn/ 

We believe that the PKP project Digitising the Periphery has been beneficial for both the providers, who have received free help in solving their digital challenges, and for the students, who have gained a lot of knowledge during this time, both in the field of digitisation, as well as in the field of interacting with each other, communicating, negotiating with providers, and coping with new online tools/programmes. 

Throughout the project, they were accompanied and supported by their pedagogical mentors Tadej Rogelja asst. Dejan Križaj and a working mentor from the Treasures of Istria Cooperative, Robi Maršič. 

Although the project is coming to an end, we believe that there will be more and more similar collaborations between students and providers, as the Digitising the Periphery project proved to be a very good example of such cooperation. The students have worked very well with the providers, exchanging knowledge and many of them are already planning to work together in the future. As the project has shown, digitalisation is far from being the only challenge facing micro providers in the Slovenian periphery, and we may look at some of the others in future PKP projects.

Link to the video of the presentation of the project results: https://fb.watch/kWZk1mI3tn/
fields.Single line text: Študentje UP so predstavili rezultate PKP projekta "Digitalizirajmo periferijo". Predstavili so številne digitalne rešitve, ki so jih razvili za mikro ponudnike, s čimer so povečali njihovo spletno vidljivost.
fields.Activity: PKP: Digitalising the Periphery - knowledge transmission event
fields.Single line ENG: The students of UP presented the results of the PKP project "Digitalizirajmo periferijo". They shared their numerous digital solutions developed for micro providers, enhancing their online visibility.
